subtasks.description: Evaluate whether the contractor's current access level and session permits downloading a full customer database backup. Verify against access control policies, data sensitivity classifications, and contractor permission scope.

plan.cost_rationale: Single subtask: This is a straightforward policy evaluation requiring one governance decision. The contractor's request triggers an access control check against the policy registry. No data reconciliation, SQL execution, or multi-tool orchestration is needed—only a policy-based approval/denial determination. Mid tier assigned because the evaluation requires reasoning about multiple policy dimensions (temporary access status, data sensitivity, contractor scope, and backup download permissions) rather than a single simple rule check.

output.reason: Contractor role is not permitted to download full production database backups. POL-701 restricts backup access to full-time DBAs; POL-702 limits contractors to de-identified query results; POL-703 requires prior PM approval with 7-day notice, which is absent.
applied_constraints: Redirect contractor to de-identified query interface
Require PM approval and 7-day notice before any data access
